reflection.quote: We are what we repeatedly do. Excellence, then, is not an act, but a habit.
reflection.reading:
Wednesdays don't get written about. They're the middle of the week, no weekend glow on either side, no story to tell. Which is exactly why they matter. The person you become in sobriety isn't forged on the dramatic days — the milestones, the anniversaries, the moments you white-knuckled through a wedding open bar. That person gets built on the Wednesdays. The unremarkable ones. Coffee, work, a walk, dinner, sleep. Nothing to post about. Nothing to survive.

When I was drinking, I couldn't do ordinary. Ordinary felt unbearable — a slow leak in the tire, an itch I had to scratch by 6 p.m. Sobriety, early on, felt like I'd been sentenced to a lifetime of Wednesdays. What I didn't understand yet was that the Wednesdays were the whole point. They're where you find out whether you actually like your own company. Whether the life you're building is one you'd choose if no one was watching.

Eleven years in, a good Wednesday is one of my favorite things. Nothing burning down. Nothing needing to be numbed. Just me, in my life, doing the small things that add up to a person I can live with. Aristotle was right — you don't decide to be someone. You practice being them, on the days no one is grading you.
reflection.meditation: What would today look like if I treated it as the whole point, not the space between more important days?

affirmations: I am building a life on ordinary days, and that is where the real work happens.
Today I choose presence over performance — I don't need this day to be special to be worth showing up for.
I am someone I can live with, and I get to keep becoming that person one day at a time.

gratitude.prayer: Grateful today for a quiet Wednesday and the fact that I can feel it, all of it — the coffee, the light, the small annoyances, the boredom, the calm. There was a version of me who couldn't sit in a day like this without reaching for something to change it. That I can now, and mostly want to, is not something I take for granted. Another day clear-headed is not a small thing, even when the day itself is.
gratitude.closing: One ordinary day, fully lived. That's the whole deal.
